summary: Iteration 23 backend testing: /api/translate + /api/help/ask + regressions. All 18 pytest tests pass. Translation guardrails (no answering questions, no English leakage) confirmed. Cache path (cached:true on repeat) works. All four supported languages (hi/ml/ta/te) return correct script ranges. Help assistant grounds answers in Karya capabilities (Profile, Connect Telegram, 6-character code) and honors user language setting. Regressions (telegram/status, dashboard/stats, insights has_data, phone verify verify_available, profile language mutation) all green.

minor.issue: ProfileIn model requires 'name' — a partial update with only {language:'ml'} returns 422. Frontend must send full profile fields on language change. Consider making name optional or add a dedicated PATCH /api/auth/profile/language endpoint.

action_items: Verify frontend Profile page's language selector sends the FULL profile payload (name, phone, etc.) when calling PUT /api/auth/profile — otherwise language switching will silently fail with 422.
Optional: expose a lightweight PATCH endpoint for language-only updates so the Profile 5-tile language selector doesn't need to reconstruct the full ProfileIn body.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/translate correctly short-circuits for target_lang='en' and validates unsupported langs (400). Cache using SHA-256(lang:text) as _id is clean and dedupes across users.
/api/help/ask has no explicit output-length cap; relies on the model to follow '3–8 short sentences'. Fine for now.
ProfileIn requires 'name' — this is a UX foot-gun for partial updates from the language switcher. See minor issue above.
seed_data_creation: Cache entry inserted into db.translations for a unique probe string (test 5) and various translations for hi/ml/ta/te 'Daily Reports'. User language reset to 'en' at teardown (verified via /api/auth/me).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file test_iteration23_translate_help.py uses a _put_profile() helper that fetches /auth/me and sends the full ProfileIn payload — reuse this pattern for language-related tests. Note: LLM latency 2-8s per call, tests use 60s timeout. User language field is reset to 'en' in module-scope fixture teardown.
